input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the standard approach:

### 1. Define each theme as a `ResourceDictionary`

```xml
<!-- Themes/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

```xml
<!-- Themes/DarkTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary ...>
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#1E1E1E</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#E0E0E0</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#4FC3F7</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

```xml
<!-- Themes/HighContrastTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary ...>
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#000000</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#FFFF00</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

### 2. Create a theme-switching service

```csharp
public static class ThemeService
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(string themeName)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;

        // Remove any existing theme dictionary
        var existing = mergedDicts.FirstOrDefault(d => d is IThemeDictionary);
        if (existing is not null)
            mergedDicts.Remove(existing);

        ResourceDictionary theme = themeName switch
        {
            "Dark"         => new Themes.DarkTheme(),
            "HighContrast" => new Themes.HighContrastTheme(),
            _              => new Themes.LightTheme(),
        };

        mergedDicts.Add(theme);

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("AppTheme", themeName);
    }
}
```

> **Tip:** Have each theme XAML class implement a marker interface (`IThemeDictionary`) so you can find and replace it easily.

### 3. Apply the saved theme at startup

In `App.xaml.cs`:

```csharp
public App()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(Preferences.Get("AppTheme", "Light"));
}
```

### 4. Build a settings picker

```xml
<!-- SettingsPage.xaml -->
<Picker Title="Theme" SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged">
    <Picker.ItemsSource>
        <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}">
            <x:String>Light</x:String>
            <x:String>Dark</x:String>
            <x:String>HighContrast</x:String>
        </x:Array>
    </Picker.ItemsSource>
</Picker>
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    if (sender is Picker p && p.SelectedItem is string theme)
        ThemeService.ApplyTheme(theme);
}
```

### 5. Consume theme colors in your pages

Use `DynamicResource` (not `StaticResource`) so values update immediately when the dictionary is swapped:

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource PageBackground}">
    <Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" Text="Hello!" />
</ContentPage>
```

**Key points:**

- **`DynamicResource`** is essential — it re-evaluates when the dictionary changes.
- **`Preferences`** persists the user's choice across app restarts.
- Keep the same `x:Key` names in every theme dictionary so the swap is seamless.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to set up themed resource dictionaries in a .NET MAUI app with light, dark, and high-contrast themes, switchable from a settings page. This is a general "how-to" question, not a code change in a repository. I'll give a concise architectural answer.
